title: acokanthera lamarckii
intro: Acokanthera lamarckii, a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, is commonly known as the 'Oudh Croton' or 'Cape Hottentots' Bells. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its cardiac glycosides, which have been shown to have a toxic effect on the heart when ingested in large quantities, but also possess potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Acokanthera lamarckii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through the extraction of its roots, leaves, or sap, which can be used to create herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, or ointments.
